Enum module: Elixir-style enumeration over List(a).

s: map(xs : List(a), f : a -> b) : List(b)
d: Applies f to each element and returns a new list of results. O(n).

s: flat_map(xs : List(a), f : a -> List(b)) : List(b)
d: Applies f to each element (f returns a list) and concatenates all results.

s: filter(xs : List(a), pred : a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Returns only the elements for which pred returns true. O(n).

s: fold(zero : b, xs : List(a), f : b -> a -> b) : b
d: Left fold over a list. f is curried: f(acc)(x) returns the new accumulator.

s: reduce(xs : List(a), f : a -> a -> a) : Option(a)
d: Like fold, but uses the first element as the initial accumulator.

s: each(xs : List(a), f : a -> b) : Unit
d: Applies f to each element for side effects and returns Unit. O(n).
Returns the number of elements in the list. O(n).

s: any(xs : List(a), pred : a -> Bool) : Bool
d: Returns true if at least one element satisfies pred. Short-circuits. O(n).

s: all(xs : List(a), pred : a -> Bool) : Bool
d: Returns true if all elements satisfy pred. Short-circuits on first failure. O(n).

s: find(xs : List(a), pred : a -> Bool) : Option(a)
d: Returns the first element satisfying pred, or None if none does. O(n).

s: group_by(xs : List(a), key : a -> k) : List((k, List(a)))
d: Groups consecutive elements with the same key into (key, [elements]) pairs.

s: zip_with(xs : List(a), ys : List(b), f : a -> b -> c) : List(c)
d: Zips two lists together applying f to corresponding elements.

s: sort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Sorts xs by cmp. Stable (preserves relative order of equal elements).

s: timsort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Sorts xs using Timsort. Stable, O(n log n) worst-case, O(n) on sorted input.

s: introsort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Sorts xs using Introsort. Unstable, O(n log n) worst-case guaranteed.

s: sort_small_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Sorts a small list (n <= 8) using optimal comparison networks.

s: chunk_by(xs : List(a), key : a -> k) : List((k, List(a)))
d: Chunks consecutive elements with the same key into (key, [elements]) pairs.

s: last(r : %{start: Int, stop: Int, step: Int}) : Option(Int)
d: Returns Some of the last element, or None if empty.

s: all(r : %{start: Int, stop: Int, step: Int}, f : Int -> Bool) : Bool
d: Returns true if all elements satisfy f.

s: any(r : %{start: Int, stop: Int, step: Int}, f : Int -> Bool) : Bool
d: Returns true if any element satisfies f.

s: sum(r : %{start: Int, stop: Int, step: Int}) : Int
d: Returns the sum of all elements in the range.
Regex module: simple regular expression engine (pure March implementation).
Test whether `pattern` matches anywhere in `string`.
Find the first match of `pattern` in `string`.
Find all non-overlapping matches of `pattern` in `string`.
Replace the first match of `pattern` in `string` with `replacement`.
Replace all non-overlapping matches of `pattern` in `string` with `replacement`.
Split `string` on matches of `pattern`.

s: ptype RegexQuant = QOne | QZeroOrMore | QOneOrMore | QOptional

d: Result module: operations on Result(a, e) = Ok(a) Err(e)
Returns true if the result is Ok.
Returns true if the result is Err.

s: expect(res : Result(a, e), msg : String) : a
d: Extracts the Ok value, panicking with the given message if Err.
Extracts the Ok value, panicking if Err.
Extracts the Err value, panicking if Ok.

s: unwrap_or(res : Result(a, e), default : a) : a
d: Returns the Ok value, or `default` if Err.

s: map(res : Result(a, e), f : a -> b) : Result(b, e)
d: Applies `f` to the Ok value, leaving Err unchanged.

s: map_err(res : Result(a, e), f : e -> f2) : Result(a, f2)
d: Applies `f` to the Err value, leaving Ok unchanged.

s: flat_map(res : Result(a, e), f : a -> Result(b, e)) : Result(b, e)
d: Applies `f` (which returns a Result) to the Ok value, flattening the result.

s: or_else(res : Result(a, e), f : e -> Result(a, f2)) : Result(a, f2)
d: Returns `f(err)` if Err, or the original Ok value.
Collects a list of Results into a Result of a list.
Converts Result(a, e) to Option(a), discarding the error.
Seq module: lazy fold-based sequences (church-encoded).
Set module: persistent hash-array-mapped-trie set.
Returns an empty set.
Returns a set containing exactly one element.
Returns true if the set contains no elements.
Returns the number of elements in the set. O(n).
Returns true if the set contains elem.
Inserts elem into the set. Returns the set unchanged if elem is already present.
Removes elem from the set. Returns the set unchanged if elem is absent.
Returns the union of two sets (all elements in either set).
Returns the intersection of two sets (elements in both sets).
Returns elements in a that are not in b (set difference a \\ b).
Returns true if a is a subset of b.
Returns all elements as a list (hash-traversal order).
Builds a set from a list of elements.
Left fold over all elements. f(acc)(elem) = new_acc.
Returns true if the two sets contain the same elements.
Sigil module: handlers for sigil expressions (~R, ~J, etc.)
Sort module: Timsort, Introsort, and AlphaDev optimal comparison networks.
Reverses a list. O(n).
Appends two lists. O(|xs|).

s: mergesort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Top-down mergesort. Stable, O(n log n).

s: sort_small_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Sorts a small list using optimal comparison networks for n ≤ 8.

s: insertion_sort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Insertion sort. Stable, O(n²).

s: timsort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Timsort. Stable, O(n log n) worst-case, O(n) on sorted input.

s: heapsort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
introsort_by(xs : List(a), cmp : a -> a -> Bool) : List(a)
d: Introsort. Unstable, O(n log n) worst-case guaranteed.

s: ptype Heap(a) = HLeaf | HNode(Int, a, Heap(a), Heap(a))

d: SortedSet module: AVL tree-based sorted set.

s: new(cmp : (a, a) -> Int) : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}
d: Creates a new empty sorted set with the given comparator.

s: add(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}, v : a) : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}
d: Adds element v to the set. No-op if already present.

s: remove(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}, v : a) : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}
d: Removes element v from the set. No-op if not present.

s: member(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}, v : a) : Bool
d: Returns true if v is in the set.

s: to_list(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}) : List(a)
d: Returns all elements in sorted order.

s: from_list(items : List(a), cmp : (a, a) -> Int) : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}
d: Builds a set from a list of elements.

s: size(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}) : Int
d: Returns the number of elements in the set.

s: min(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}) : Option(a)
d: Returns the smallest element, or None if empty.

s: max(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}) : Option(a)
d: Returns the largest element, or None if empty.

s: union(a : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}, b : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}) : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}
d: Returns a set containing all elements in either a or b.

s: intersect(a : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}, b : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}) : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}
d: Returns a set containing only elements in both a and b.

s: difference(a : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}, b : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}) : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}
d: Returns a set of elements in a that are not in b.

s: fold(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}, acc : b, f : (b, a) -> b) : b
d: Folds over the set in sorted order: f(acc, element) -> acc.

s: is_empty(s : %{cmp: (a, a) -> Int, tree: Tree(a)}) : Bool
d: Returns true if the set is empty.

s: subset(a : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}, b : %{cmp: (k, k) -> Int, tree: Tree(k)}) : Bool
d: Returns true if a is a subset of b (every element of a is in b).

s: Tree(a) = Leaf | Node(Tree(a), a, Tree(a), Int)

d: Stats module: descriptive statistics on List(Float).
Sum of all elements. Returns 0.0 for empty list.
Number of elements.
Arithmetic mean. Panics on empty list.
Minimum value. Panics on empty list.
Maximum value. Panics on empty list.
Percentile using linear interpolation.
Median (50th percentile). Panics on empty list.
Sample variance (denominator n-1).
Population variance (denominator n).
Sample standard deviation. Returns 0.0 for single element. Panics on empty.
Population standard deviation.
Mode: the most frequently occurring value.
Sample covariance of two equal-length lists.
Pearson correlation coefficient.
Simple linear regression: y = slope * x + intercept.
Mean, returning Err on empty list instead of panicking.
Sample variance, returning Err on empty list.
Sample standard deviation, returning Err on empty or single-element list.
Minimum value, returning Err on empty list.
Maximum value, returning Err on empty list.

Set an environment variable.

Return the OS-level PID of the current process as an Int.
Terminate the process with the given exit code.

Task module: Elixir-style lightweight async tasks for one-off concurrent work.

stdlib/tls.march — TLS (Transport Layer Security) support for March.

d: Returns true if both elements satisfy their respective predicates.

Unzips a list of pairs into a pair of lists.
